title: alpinia fasciculata
intro: Alpinia fasciculata, also known as the dwarf ginger, is a species of flowering plant in the ginger family. This plant is native to the tropical forests of Borneo and is valued for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Alpinia fasciculata include reducing fever, relieving pain, and aiding in digestion. Alpinia fasciculata can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, making it a popular ingredient in traditional medicine.
